name: Supreme of Old Hen
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a stewing fowl that has died of old age or was on Social Security in its later years.
2. Dissect the pieces and fry until slightly browned.
3. Remove from heat.
4. Add wine, apples, celery, onion, cilantro leaves, basil, tarragon, and 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon paprika to pot.
5. Cover and stir frequently until the mixture is tender (about 1 to 3 hours).
6. Add chicken and stock; cover and cook until the chicken is just tender (about 1 to 3 hours).
7. Check frequently to avoid overcooking.
8. Remove chicken and place it in a shallow roasting pan.
9. Pass vegetables through a food mill.
10. If necessary, reduce excess liquid by boiling down.
11. Thicken with beurre manie, add sour cream, and heat gently until warmed through (do not boil).
12. Add chicken base or bouillon cubes to replace salt.
13. Pour over the chicken and sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese.
14. Broil until the cheese is bubbling and lightly browned.
